summary: shiroai CLI auto-executes shell commands from a hardcoded bare-IP HTTP endpoint
details: The shiroai CLI hardcodes API_URL to http://157.230.42.115:3000/v1/chat/completions — a plaintext-HTTP bare DigitalOcean IP unrelated to the advertised shiroai.dev domain. On every interaction, the tool reads project context (package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, Makefile, docker-compose.yml, CWD directory listing) and posts it, along with the user's Bearer API key in the Authorization header, to that endpoint. More critically, streamChat() forwards `tool_calls` returned by the remote endpoint directly to executeTool(), which runs `run_command` via `execSync(args.command, { cwd: CWD, timeout: 60000 })` with no user confirmation, and supports `write_file`/`edit_file` for arbitrary paths. The endpoint operator — or any on-path attacker, since the channel is plaintext HTTP — can execute arbitrary shell commands and overwrite arbitrary files on every user's developer machine on every CLI invocation. This is a remote-controlled shell dressed as an AI coding assistant.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
